evals.name: new-feature-worktree-with-existing-directory
evals.prompt: I need to work on a new authentication feature. Set up an isolated worktree for it on a branch called feature/auth.
evals.expected_output: Worktree created in the existing worktree directory after verifying .gitignore, project dependencies installed, baseline tests run, and final location reported
expectations: Announces at start that it is using the using-git-worktrees skill
Checks for existing .worktrees/ and worktrees/ directories before asking the user
If .worktrees/ directory exists, uses it over worktrees/
Verifies .gitignore contains the worktree directory pattern before creating the worktree
If worktree directory is not in .gitignore, adds it and commits the change before proceeding
Runs git worktree add with -b flag to create a new branch
Checks for .env* files in the source project root and asks user how to handle them (copy, symlink, or skip)
Auto-detects project type from lockfiles/config files and runs appropriate install command with frozen-lockfile or ci flags where available
Runs project tests to verify a clean baseline after setup
Reports final worktree path, test results, and readiness status
anti_expectations: Does not skip .gitignore verification for project-local worktree directories
Does not assume a worktree directory location without checking existing directories and CLAUDE.md first
Does not proceed past failing baseline tests without reporting failures and asking the user
Does not silently skip env file handling when .env* files exist in the source root

evals.name: worktree-no-existing-directory-global-path
evals.prompt: Set up a worktree for a bugfix branch called fix/memory-leak. There's no worktrees directory in this project yet.
evals.expected_output: Checks for existing directories and CLAUDE.md preference, asks user to choose directory location, creates worktree in chosen location, runs setup and verification
expectations: Announces at start that it is using the using-git-worktrees skill
Checks for .worktrees/ directory first, then worktrees/ directory
Checks CLAUDE.md for a worktree directory preference before asking the user
When no directory exists and no CLAUDE.md preference, presents the user with directory options including .worktrees/ (project-local, hidden) and ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/<project>/ (global)
If user chooses global directory (~/.config/superpowers/worktrees), does NOT perform .gitignore verification
Detects project name using git rev-parse --show-toplevel and basename
Runs git worktree add with the correct path based on the chosen directory location
Auto-detects and runs the appropriate dependency install command
anti_expectations: Does not skip the CLAUDE.md check when no existing directory is found
Does not perform .gitignore verification for global (non-project-local) worktree directories
Does not hardcode setup commands without checking for project-specific lockfiles and config files

evals.name: worktree-with-env-files-and-failing-tests
evals.prompt: Create a worktree for feature/dashboard. The project has .env and .env.local files and I want them symlinked.
evals.expected_output: Worktree created, env files symlinked from source root, dependencies installed, baseline test results reported with pass/fail status
expectations: Announces at start that it is using the using-git-worktrees skill
Follows directory selection priority: checks existing directories, then CLAUDE.md, then asks user
Verifies .gitignore for project-local worktree directory
Detects .env* files in the source project root
Since user specified symlink preference, creates symlinks from source root env files to the worktree directory
Uses ln -s to create symlinks pointing back to the original .env* files
Runs appropriate dependency install command based on project type detection
Runs baseline tests after setup is complete
If baseline tests fail, reports the failures and asks whether to proceed or investigate rather than silently continuing
anti_expectations: Does not copy env files when user requested symlink
Does not skip baseline test verification
Does not proceed with failing tests without explicitly reporting and asking the user
Does not skip env file handling when .env* files exist
